source_title: How to Add Pagination in Word | Step-by-Step Tutorial 📄🔢

concepts.name: Pagination
concepts.definition: The process of adding page numbers to a document to help readers navigate the content and maintain a professional structure.

concepts.name: Header
concepts.definition: The top margin area of a page where consistent information (like page numbers) can be placed across multiple pages.

concepts.name: Footer
concepts.definition: The bottom margin area of a page typically used for page numbering, citations, or dates.

procedures.name: Adding Page Numbers in Microsoft Word

steps.explanation: The Insert tab contains all tools for adding external elements like tables, images, and page numbers.
This opens the pagination menu to choose the position and style of the numbers.

steps.action: Select the position (Top of Page or Bottom of Page)
steps.explanation: Determines whether the number appears in the Header or the Footer.
Allows the user to select alignment (Left, Center, Right) or specific formatting styles.
Returns the user to the main document editing mode.

gotchas.description: Accidentally editing the main body text while in Header/Footer mode
gotchas.context: When the user is trying to style the page number.
gotchas.solution: Double-click the main body of the document or click 'Close Header and Footer' in the ribbon to return to normal editing.
